title: Midday Update
commentary: Oil in total capitulation—WTI collapsing 11% to $84.22 as ceasefire momentum builds, erasing nearly all of last week's war premium in a single session. Equities finally catching bid: Dow +0.5% at 47,985, S&P +0.4%, Nasdaq +0.6% as the energy drag transforms into tailwind. European bourses ripping—DAX +2.5%, FTSE +1.9% on relief. Precious metals paradoxically rallying with gold +1.4% to $5,210 and silver +2.5%—the uncertainty hedge still has believers. Dollar sliding 0.6% as risk-on flows dominate.

title: Morning Update
commentary: Oil in freefall—WTI crashing 7.7% to $87.45 as markets fully price in Trump's Iran war 'nearly complete' messaging, with Brent mirroring the bloodbath at -7.7%. Equities struggling to find direction despite the energy relief—Dow -0.5% at 47,498, S&P -0.4%—as traders question whether the ceasefire optimism is premature. Precious metals catching a strong bid with gold +1.2% to $5,203 and silver +2.1% as the geopolitical uncertainty trade lives on. Dollar sliding 0.4% as safe-haven flows reverse. Bitcoin holding $70K territory as crypto shows resilience.

title: Market Open
commentary: Oil whipsawing at the open as Hegseth promises Tuesday will be the 'most intense day of strikes' on Iran—crude bouncing back toward $90 after yesterday's 5% plunge on ceasefire hopes. Futures flat as traders await clarity on whether Trump's 'war nearly complete' rhetoric holds or if escalation resumes. Asia bounced hard overnight—Nikkei +2.9%, Hang Seng +2.2%—while European stocks surged 2% on relief. Gold catching a bid at $5,187 (+0.9%) as geopolitical whiplash keeps safe-haven demand alive. Bitcoin rallying to $70.5K as crypto decouples from risk-off narrative.

title: Market Close
commentary: Oil crashed back from near $120 after Trump signaled the Iran conflict could end soon, sending crude tumbling 5%+ and sparking a relief rally that pushed equities back into the green. S&P 500 closed +0.83%, Nasdaq +1.38% as risk appetite returned—a stunning reversal from the morning's bloodbath. Asian markets remained battered with Tokyo's Nikkei nursing a 5.2% loss overnight. Gold eased 0.3% as safe-haven flows reversed while silver outperformed with a 2.4% gain. Crypto under pressure—Bitcoin flat near $69K, Ethereum down 5%. Markets now hang on every headline from the Middle East.

title: Late Afternoon
commentary: Heading into the final hour with losses contained—Dow -0.8% at 47,104, a far cry from the -2.1% opening disaster. Nasdaq nearly flat at -0.08%, proving once again that tech is the cockroach that survives everything. Oil holding below $100 at $98.80, giving bulls just enough cover to defend positions. Mining stocks flashing sell signals per IBD as gold's +2.4% rally looks stretched. Traders positioning cautiously ahead of potential overnight Iran headlines—nobody wants to be a hero before the close.

title: Afternoon Session
commentary: Rally fading into the afternoon as markets give back some gains—Dow settling around -1.0% at 47,022, Nasdaq slipping back to -0.25% after briefly turning green. Oil stabilizing below $100 at $98.70 but traders remain nervous about overnight headline risk from the Middle East. S&P -0.6% with the intraday bounce losing steam as institutional selling resumes. Still a remarkable recovery from the opening chaos—what looked like potential panic has morphed into orderly de-risking. Bitcoin pulling back to $68,635 (+3.7%) as crypto gives up some outperformance.

title: Early Afternoon
commentary: The comeback is real—Nasdaq flips positive (+0.2%) as oil's retreat below $100 unleashes aggressive dip-buying. Dow cuts losses to just -0.5% at 47,252, a stunning reversal from the -2.1% opening abyss. WTI crude settling around $98.50 after touching $105 overnight—G-7 reserve release coordination calming nerves. S&P -0.2% with tech leading the charge back; the Iran risk premium already fading from morning panic levels. Bears caught offside as 'buy the dip' mentality proves alive and well despite geopolitical chaos.

title: Noon Update
commentary: Relief rally accelerating as oil retreats below $100—Dow now -0.9% at 47,055, clawing back half its morning losses. The crude dip to $99.40 from session highs of $105 sparking a 'worst case avoided' narrative per Nomura. S&P -0.5%, Nasdaq nearly flat at -0.15% as growth names lead the bounce. Nomura strategist calling markets 'priced for absolute chaos' but predicting de-escalation—dip buyers emboldened. Bitcoin consolidating gains at $68,878 (+4.1%), with crypto outperforming traditional safe havens on the session.

title: Midday Update
commentary: Stocks staging a tentative recovery from the opening rout—Dow claws back to -1.1% at 46,987 after trading as low as -2.1%, with transport stocks bearing the brunt of oil pain. S&P -0.8%, Nasdaq -0.5% as tech proves more resilient than cyclicals. Treasury yields spiking on stagflation concerns—10Y pushing toward 4.8% as traders price in higher inflation without growth. Oil holding above $100 at $101.80, down from session highs of $105 amid G-7 reserve release speculation. Crypto extending its surprising rally: Bitcoin +5.1% to $69,275, leading the 'digital gold' narrative as actual gold hits $5,328 (+3.1%).

title: Morning Update
commentary: Markets finding a fragile floor after the opening bloodbath—Dow pares losses to -1.5% at 46,803 after touching -2.1% at session lows. S&P -1.3%, Nasdaq -1.2% as dip buyers tentatively emerge on G-7 strategic reserve chatter. Oil retreating from $105 highs to $101.50 but remains up 14% on the day—the Iran conflict showing no signs of de-escalation. Crypto surprisingly resilient: Bitcoin +4.6% to $69,015, Ethereum +4.3% as digital assets decouple from risk-off equity trade. Gold +2.8% to $5,312 benefiting from safe-haven flows while dollar surges 0.9% to 100.7.
